change_comments.message: I am concerned about the complexity of this after just revieing the first file. Maybe we need to touch base and have a little chat about this epic again...

I have some concerns about 'feature toggle' maybe we need to discuss.
Apart from that  just some minor cleanup comments
Resolve merger conflict before merge, but looks good to me.

inline_comments.message: NB: can u explicitly specifier the access modifier.
NB: access modifier here as well.
not required as anyways beans are injected using constructor injection.

add explicit asserts wherever we are verifying.
applies for all the tests.

the class is already call CacheHandler so I dont think you need to use the word 'cache' in method names.

Also typically with cache one uses words like 'add' and 'remove'. Finally, be clear WHAT you add to the cache, not the event!


this class shouldn't realy know about the event. that is just the current way of communicating subscr. details . I think you should only depend on the relevant two attributes of the subscription.

suggested name and parameters: 
addSubscriptionDetails(subscriptionId, predicates)
yu are ONYL using teh predicates so I think I woudl pass in only that
why sushc a long name, do we have otehr predicates ?!

what is the difference between a requestPredicate and a CmSubscrptionPredicate?!
maybe you need better name for teh latter?

k ? not i, or mayeb it is not an iterator then use longer name.
Maybe  is it needed at all ?
looks to me like you are storing the exact same data in a slightly different structure object, is it needed at all?

I understand what you are doing but this method is looking bulky ??
can you think to break it down to multiple methods to improve readability ???

NB: targetFilters OR targetCmHandleIds ..
I think we decided to call it targetCmHandleIds at this level.. right ??
try to use some test instance of the map and not the real one.
NB: Id or id for the testware.
this is still targetFilter from the event, i called it targetCmHandles when we are talking about the cmHandles for the DMI

Legacy : do you think now we should get rid of this ??
I guess we introduced when the model loader framework was not stable.
What do you think ??
Also we can take this as a separate patch as well. Not necessary to be part of this.

NB: can also call the method here.
method name to me is descriptive enough.

I think this loop is to populate dmiCmNotificationSubscriptionDetailsPerDmi , so maybe we can extract this bit of code to a private method.
My suggestion : populateDmiCmNotificationSubscriptionDetailsPerDmi 😊
You can always come up with a better name.
NB: want to use the real instance here ??
to be discussed with other team members and based on decision we will take action.

do we really need a feature toggle ie. do we BREAK anything if it is simply there (but not yet released/documented)

A feature toggle mean you need to test 2 code paths everywhere you use it!...
I think we diccused this before (for other caches): I think you can use just Map heer which wil make testing easier as you can 'mock' it using a groovy map [:]
NB: could set the size to 1
question: can we add a constructor taking the reuired properties below or is this generated and beyond our control ? (I suspect it is out of our control)
see what I mean, you are only testing one code path here...
can we be more strict here?

use Groovy Map Contructors e.g
def yangModelCmHandle1 = new YangModelCmHandle(id:'ch1',dmiServiceName:'dmi-1)
can you extract method(s) withe meaningfull name(S) to improve reability please

this one I can handle Toine , I already commented regarding this.
I introduced this toggle switch when we newly introduced ModelLoader functionality and we were having issues around it.
So ericsson team was having some deployment issues coz of this. So this was the quickest solution at that time. But now since functionality around model loader seems to be stable. Now is a good time to get rid of this.
I will create a ticket and will post here to handle it.

do you need this to be injected by Spring ??  I dont think so.
and there is no bean already defined for this as far as I see.

better to pass the reference from the method itself from where its called.
